fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 45-year-old Hispanic male with chronic psychotic disorder with moderate symptoms.
fields.Subjective: Mr. Ramirez attended appointment with brother who supervises medications. Brother confirms patient takes ziprasidone 80 mg twice daily with meals via supervision, no missed doses. Despite good adherence, continues experiencing moderate symptom burden. Reports daily auditory hallucinations of voices arguing. Endorses paranoid thoughts that neighbors watch his activities. Sleep disrupted at 5-6 hours nightly. Appetite decreased, weight down 5 lbs to 161 lbs. Not working, receives disability. Lives with brother who provides support. Limited activities. Brother concerned about ongoing symptoms. Mood 'nervous' rated 4/10. Denies substance use. Safety: No SI/HI. (Assessed by Dr. Fernando Chavez, MD, Psychiatrist)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Middle-aged male, fair grooming, appears anxious

fields.Current_Medications: Ziprasidone 80 mg PO BID with food
fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Other chronic psychotic disorder with persistent symptoms and anxiety.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives with brother; unemployed, on disability; limited activities; ongoing symptoms.
Assessment_and_Plan: Already on maximum ziprasidone with ongoing symptoms.
Will add SSRI for anxiety and mood.
fields.Author: Note written by Dr. Fernando Chavez, MD, Psychiatrist — Hermanos Mental Health, 3421 Brotherhood Way, El Paso, TX 79901, Phone: (555) 447-8821, Fax: (555) 447-8822
